score_rationales.usability: Band 90+: one clearly primary action, conventional layout, visible active nav state, nothing destructive unguarded.
score_rationales.cognitive_load: Band 90+: two fields, one decision, no mental work the UI could do.
score_rationales.visual_layout: Band 90+: grouping matches semantics; single hierarchy passes the squint test.
score_rationales.accessibility: Band 80-89: measured contrast passes on CTA (6.7:1) and helper text (5.31:1); all inputs visibly labeled; focus states unverifiable from a static image.
score_rationales.content: Band 80-89: outcome-named CTA and useful helper copy; truncated account label in the header is the only blemish.
score_rationales.trust_persuasion: Band 80-89: no dark patterns; no high-stakes ask on this screen requiring trust signals.

one_big_thing: Nothing structural to fix on this screen; the lowest-value real improvement is confirming saves visibly, which can only be evaluated with the post-save screen.
clean_checks: WCAG-3.3.2: both inputs have persistent visible labels
WCAG-1.4.3: primary CTA 6.7:1 and helper text 5.31:1, measured passes
NRM-06: active nav item differentiated by weight and accent bar, not color alone
RF-08: a single primary action; Cancel is visibly secondary
Not auditable statically: save feedback (NLS-01), focus indicators, keyboard order — verify live
